title: Fernandezia squarrosa
taxonomy: Fernandezia squarrosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Fernandezia, which is a group of orchids native to South America. Within the Orchidaceae family, Fernandezia squarrosa is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cestroideae. The plant is native to the Andean region of South America, where it grows in the wild. This classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics and traits of the Fernandezia squarrosa species.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat wounds and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Fernandezia squarrosa may also have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its full range of medicinal uses.
